title: Service Outcomes, Boundaries, and Server Lifecycle · Language Lens: Client Server Intermediary Boundary
summary: A civic incident API has endpoints but no stated user outcome, ownership boundary, startup contract, dependency readiness, shutdown policy, or evidence connecting a request to durable effect. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo client server intermediary boundary increment in service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace user agent, client, proxy, gateway, origin handler, dependency, and response roles without assigning one component evidence it cannot observe.
Define a server outcome with user, client, operator, security, and accessibility evidence instead of treating endpoint count as success.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Client Server Intermediary Boundary · Theory Client Server Intermediary Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7405 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate listener liveness, dependency readiness, and draining state; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace user agent, client, proxy, gateway, origin handler, dependency, and response roles without assigning one component evidence it cannot observe.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Client Server Intermediary Boundary contract for the supplied data.
